metadata.note: Output used as-is without manual revision or selection.

causal_texts: The glass slipped from her hand. It fell to the floor. It broke into many pieces. She swept them up carefully.
The alarm clock rang at six in the morning. He got out of bed slowly. He walked to the kitchen and made coffee. He sat down and drank it quietly.
Dark clouds gathered over the town. Rain began to fall heavily. The streets quickly filled with water. People ran indoors to stay dry.
She planted tomato seeds in her garden. She watered them every single day. Green sprouts appeared after one week. By summer she had ripe red tomatoes.
The dog spotted a squirrel in the yard. It ran across the grass at full speed. The squirrel climbed up a tall oak tree. The dog barked at the tree for several minutes.
He studied hard for the math exam all week. He arrived at school early on test day. He answered every question on the paper carefully. He received the highest score in the class.
The baker mixed flour, eggs, and sugar together. She poured the batter into a round pan. She placed the pan inside the hot oven. An hour later a golden cake was ready.
The boy climbed onto his new bicycle. He pedaled down the steep hill very fast. He hit a small rock on the road. He fell off the bike and scraped his knee.
Snow fell heavily throughout the entire night. By morning a thick white blanket covered everything. The children ran outside with their sleds. They spent all day playing on the hills.
She opened her laptop and began to write. The words flowed easily for several hours. She finished the last chapter before midnight. She saved the file and closed the computer.
The cat knocked a vase off the shelf. Water spilled all over the wooden floor. The flowers scattered across the wet surface. The owner grabbed a towel and cleaned the mess.
He forgot to set his alarm the night before. He woke up an hour later than usual. He skipped breakfast and ran to the bus stop. He arrived at work twenty minutes late.
The teacher handed out blank sheets of paper. The students picked up their colored pencils. They drew pictures of their favorite animals. The teacher hung the drawings on the classroom wall.
A strong wind blew through the campsite at night. It knocked over the tent poles completely. The family woke up under collapsed fabric. They rebuilt the tent together at sunrise.
She noticed a stray kitten hiding under her car. She put a bowl of warm milk on the ground. The kitten came out slowly and began drinking. She decided to take the kitten home.
The river rose after three days of heavy rain. Water began to flood the nearby farmland. The farmers moved their animals to higher ground. Emergency crews arrived and built temporary barriers.
He loaded all the boxes into the moving truck. He drove across the city to the new apartment. He carried each box up three flights of stairs. By evening the entire truck was finally empty.
The power went out during the thunderstorm last night. The house became completely dark and very quiet. She found a flashlight in the kitchen drawer. She lit several candles around the living room.
The chef chopped fresh vegetables on a wooden board. He tossed them into a large hot pan. He stirred the vegetables with a wooden spoon. He served the dish on a warm white plate.
She received a letter from an old friend overseas. She sat down on the porch to read it. The letter described travels through several countries. She wrote a long reply that same afternoon.
The mechanic lifted the car on the metal jack. He removed the flat tire from the wheel. He replaced it with a brand new spare tire. He lowered the car back to the ground carefully.
The children gathered sticks and dry leaves outside. They stacked them in a pile near the firepit. Their father lit the pile with a single match. They roasted marshmallows over the warm flames.

She signed up for a beginner swimming class in May. She practiced in the pool three times a week. Her technique improved steadily over the summer months. By September she could swim fifty laps without stopping.
The bird gathered small twigs from the garden below. It carried them up to a branch in the tree. It wove the twigs into a round nest shape. Soon three small blue eggs appeared inside the nest.

He accidentally left the garden hose running all night. A large puddle formed in the middle of the yard. The neighbor's dog came over and splashed in it. He turned off the hose and apologized to his neighbor.
The musician tuned her guitar before the evening show. She walked onto the stage in front of hundreds. She played her newest song for the first time. The audience stood up and clapped at the end.
The ice cream cone began to melt in the sun. Sticky drops ran down the little girl's hand. She tried to lick them before they reached her elbow. Her mother handed her a napkin just in time.

The old bridge began to crack during the winter frost. Engineers came to inspect the damage in early spring. They closed the bridge to all traffic for safety reasons. A new steel bridge was built by the following year.

She mixed paint colors on a wooden palette carefully. She dipped her brush and applied it to the canvas. She worked on the painting for nearly five straight hours. She stepped back and admired the finished landscape scene.

The waiter brought the menus to the corner table. The couple spent several minutes deciding on their meals. They ordered pasta and a bottle of red wine together. They finished dinner and left a generous tip behind.
noncausal_texts: A violin is played with a bow. A flute is played by blowing air. A drum is played by striking it. A harp is played by plucking strings.

Mars appears reddish due to iron oxide on its surface. Jupiter has a massive storm called the Great Red Spot. Saturn is surrounded by visible rings made of ice. Neptune has the fastest winds in the solar system.

Brazil is the largest country in South America by area. Kenya is located on the eastern coast of Africa. Sweden is a Scandinavian country in northern Europe. Peru is home to the ancient ruins of Machu Picchu.

Cotton is a natural fiber harvested from plants. Silk is produced by silkworms in their cocoon stage. Wool comes from the fleece of domesticated sheep. Linen is made from the fibers of the flax plant.
A hexagon has exactly six sides and six angles. A triangle has three sides and three interior angles. A pentagon is a polygon with five straight sides. An octagon is a shape with eight equal sides.

Pandas eat mostly bamboo in the mountain forests of China. Koalas feed primarily on eucalyptus leaves in Australia. Sloths hang from trees in Central and South American rainforests. Penguins live in cold climates across the Southern Hemisphere.

The Nile is a major river flowing through northeastern Africa. The Amazon carries more water than any other river on Earth. The Danube passes through ten countries across central Europe. The Ganges is considered a sacred river in India.

Oak trees can live for several hundred years or more. Maple trees produce a sweet sap used for syrup. Birch trees have distinctive white and peeling bark. Willow trees often grow near rivers and ponds.

Spanish is the official language of more than twenty countries. Mandarin Chinese has the most native speakers worldwide. Arabic is written and read from right to left. Portuguese is widely spoken in both Brazil and Portugal.

Copper is an excellent conductor of electrical current. Gold is highly resistant to corrosion and tarnishing over time. Iron is the most commonly used metal in construction worldwide. Aluminum is lightweight and often used in aircraft manufacturing.

Chess originated in India around the sixth century. Checkers is played on the same board as chess. Go is an ancient strategy game from China. Backgammon involves both dice rolling and strategic movement.

A marathon covers a distance of roughly forty-two kilometers. Fencing is a combat sport using thin bladed weapons. Table tennis uses a lightweight hollow ball in play. Archery requires a bow and a set of arrows.

The heart pumps blood throughout the entire human body. The lungs take in oxygen and release carbon dioxide. The liver filters toxins from the bloodstream continuously. The kidneys regulate fluid balance and remove waste products.

Limestone is a sedimentary rock composed mainly of calcium carbonate. Granite is an igneous rock found in mountain ranges worldwide. Marble is a metamorphic rock valued for sculpture and building. Sandstone forms from compressed sand grains over long time periods.

A cello produces a deep and rich musical tone. A piccolo plays some of the highest notes in an orchestra. A tuba is one of the largest brass instruments available. A clarinet uses a single reed to produce its sound.

Tokyo is the capital city of Japan on Honshu island. Canberra serves as the capital city of Australia. Ottawa is the national capital of Canada in Ontario province. Cairo is the capital and largest city of Egypt.

Dolphins communicate using a series of clicks and whistles. Elephants can hear sounds over very long distances. Bats navigate in darkness using echolocation to find prey. Whales sing complex songs that travel through ocean water.

A sonnet is a poem that contains exactly fourteen lines. A haiku consists of three lines with a set syllable count. A limerick is a humorous poem with five lines total. A ballad tells a story through rhythmic verse and song.

Broccoli is a green vegetable rich in vitamin C content. Carrots contain beta-carotene which supports healthy eyesight. Spinach provides a significant amount of dietary iron per serving. Sweet potatoes are high in fiber and vitamin A content.

The Great Wall stretches across northern China for thousands of kilometers. The Colosseum in Rome once held fifty thousand spectators at events. The Taj Mahal in India is built entirely of white marble. Machu Picchu sits high in the Andes Mountains of Peru.

Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun in our system. Venus rotates in the opposite direction of most other planets. Uranus tilts on its side as it orbits around the Sun. Mars has two small moons named Phobos and Deimos.

A bicycle has two wheels connected by a metal frame. A tricycle has three wheels and is common for young children. A unicycle has only one wheel and requires strong balance. A tandem bicycle is designed for two riders sitting in line.

Cinnamon comes from the inner bark of tropical tree species. Black pepper is the dried fruit of a flowering vine. Turmeric is a bright yellow spice ground from a root. Cumin seeds are harvested from a plant in the parsley family.

Lake Baikal in Russia contains about twenty percent of surface freshwater. Lake Titicaca sits on the border of Bolivia and Peru. Lake Victoria is the largest lake by area in Africa. The Caspian Sea is the largest enclosed body of water worldwide.

Wool socks keep feet warm in cold winter weather conditions. A silk scarf feels smooth and lightweight against the skin. Leather gloves protect hands while allowing flexible finger movement. A cotton shirt is comfortable to wear in warm summer temperatures.

Owls can rotate their heads nearly two hundred seventy degrees. Eagles have eyesight several times sharper than human vision. Hummingbirds can hover in place by beating wings very rapidly. Parrots can learn to mimic human speech and various sounds.

A thermometer measures the temperature of air or liquids. A barometer is used to measure atmospheric air pressure. An anemometer measures the speed of wind in an area. A hygrometer determines the level of humidity in the air.

Bread is a staple food in many European countries and cultures. Rice serves as the primary grain in much of East Asia. Corn is a widely grown crop across North and South America. Millet is an important cereal grain in parts of Africa.

A submarine travels underwater using ballast tanks for depth control. A helicopter can take off and land vertically without a runway. A sailboat moves across water using wind caught in fabric sails. A hot air balloon rises because heated air is lighter.

The piano has eighty-eight keys arranged in a repeating pattern. The guitar typically has six strings tuned to specific notes. The banjo usually features four or five metal strings. The accordion produces sound by compressing and expanding its bellows.
